title: cyperus inauratus
intro: Cyperus inauratus, also known as the yellow papyrus sedge, is a type of aquatic plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive yellow or golden flowers and long, thin stems. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Cyperus inauratus include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
